data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T163204-5101d3bf/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T163204-5101d3bf/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T163204-5101d3bf/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T163204-5101d3bf/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T163204-5101d3bf/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-d0be829d">
    <title>feat(bead): rev-bound auto-invalidation for push_failed cooldowns</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  Cooldowns survive their cause. When a code fix lands on origin
  that resolves the cause of a cooldown (e.g., a data race fix that
  was blocking pre-push tests), affected cooldowns remain active
  until wall-clock expiry — typically hours after the fix is
  available. Drain idles even though the world has moved on.

  Sister bead R1 removes push_failed cooldowns entirely (the most
  egregious case). This bead handles the GENERAL pattern: when
  origin/main advances past the rev at which a cooldown was set,
  the cooldown's "world-state" is stale and should auto-clear.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1524-1533 activeRetryCooldown is
    purely wall-clock: parses execute-loop-retry-after, returns
    retryAfter.After(now).
  - SetExecutionCooldown does not record the base-rev at which the
    cooldown was set.
  - There is no auto-invalidation hook anywhere.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Extend SetExecutionCooldown to record the base-rev:
     - cli/internal/bead/store.go: SetExecutionCooldown gains a
       baseRev parameter; writes execute-loop-cooldown-base-rev to
       Extra alongside execute-loop-retry-after.
     - All callers updated to pass current main HEAD or attempt
       base-rev.

  2. Extend activeRetryCooldown to check rev advance:
     - If execute-loop-cooldown-base-rev is set AND current
       origin/main HEAD has advanced past that rev, return
       active=false (cooldown auto-cleared).
     - "Advanced past" means: current HEAD is a descendant of the
       cooldown base-rev OR current HEAD != cooldown base-rev (use
       git merge-base or simple rev equality, depending on
       implementation cost).
     - Cache the current origin HEAD per ready-queue evaluation pass
       to avoid one git read per bead.

  3. Document the contract:
     - Add comment at activeRetryCooldown explaining the rev-bound
       semantics.
     - Reference TD-031 §5 invariant.

  Tests:
     - TestActiveRetryCooldown_RevBoundAutoClears: set cooldown at
       rev A, advance HEAD to rev B (descendant), assert
       activeRetryCooldown returns false.
     - TestActiveRetryCooldown_SameRevStillActive: set cooldown at
       rev A, leave HEAD at rev A, assert active=true.
     - TestActiveRetryCooldown_NoBaseRevFallsBackToWallClock:
       legacy cooldowns without base-rev still work via wall-clock
       only (backward-compat).

NON-SCOPE
  - Hooks for arbitrary "cause resolved" detection beyond rev-advance
    (e.g., specific test now passing). Out of scope; rev-advance is
    the simple proxy.
  - Migrating existing cooldowns to record base-rev (they expire on
    wall-clock; new ones get the new field).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead R1 (push_failed no cooldown) reduces the impact of
    this bead but does not eliminate the need: other cooldowns
    (no_changes, no_viable_provider, etc.) still benefit from
    rev-bound auto-clear when applicable.
  - Sister bead R2 (bulk clear-cooldowns) is the manual escape
    valve; this is the automatic version.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/bead/store.go SetExecutionCooldown writes execute-loop-cooldown-base-rev alongside execute-loop-retry-after.
2. cli/internal/bead/store.go activeRetryCooldown checks the base-rev: if origin/main HEAD has advanced past the recorded base-rev, returns active=false even if wall-clock has not expired.
3. The current origin HEAD is read once per ready-queue evaluation pass (cached, not per-bead).
4. TestActiveRetryCooldown_RevBoundAutoClears asserts cooldown clears when HEAD advances.
5. TestActiveRetryCooldown_SameRevStillActive asserts cooldown stays active when HEAD has not advanced.
6. TestActiveRetryCooldown_NoBaseRevFallsBackToWallClock asserts legacy cooldowns without base-rev still work.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:bead, area:agent, kind:feature, prevention</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="2f3d6eb648aacd8f60ac9a530cd689a9e9766959" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T163204-5101d3bf"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All checks pass and the commit is made. Let me verify the AC coverage:

**AC1** ✅ `SetExecutionCooldown` now writes `execute-loop-cooldown-base-rev` (via `ExtraCooldownBaseRev`) when `baseRev != ""`

**AC2** ✅ `activeRetryCooldown` checks `ExtraCooldownBaseRev`: if `originHead != "" && originHead != baseRev`, returns `active=false`

**AC3** ✅ `classifyLifecycleQueue` calls `originMainHead(s.workingDir())` once before the loop, passes the result to each `activeRetryCooldown` call

**AC4** ✅ `TestActiveRetryCooldown_RevBoundAutoClears` — asserts cooldown clears when HEAD advances

**AC5** ✅ `TestActiveRetryCooldown_SameRevStillActive` — asserts cooldown stays active when HEAD unchanged

**AC6** ✅ `TestActiveRetryCooldown_NoBaseRevFallsBackToWallClock` — asserts legacy cooldowns without base-rev use wall-clock only

**AC7** ✅ `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/...` — both pass

**AC8** ✅ `lefthook run pre-commit` — all checks pass
